sections.paragraph:
to open, specially with עֵינַיִם the eyes, 2Ki 4:35 19:16 Job 27:19, etc.; once used of the ears ( Isa 42:20). To open (one’s) eyes upon any one (followed by עַלּ) is to observe him diligently, Job 14:3 to care for him, Zec 12:4. Absol. to open the eyes, or to have them open, i.q. to be vigilant, diligent, opp. to lazy, drowsy, Pro 20:13. Elsewhere God is said to open any one’s eyes, in a double sense-(a) to restore sight to the blind, 2Ki 6:17, 20 2Ki 6:20 Psa 146:8 Isa 42:7.-(b) to enable to see things, which otherwise are hidden from the eyes of mortals, Gen 21:19 compare Niphal.

Niphal, to be opened (used of the eyes), Isa 35:5. Metaph. Gen 3:5, .

Derived nouns, פֶּקַח-פְּקַח־קֹוחַ.
